text: The surgeon performs a lateral docking on the left side, followed by a crossover maneuver, and then repositions the surgical ports for a top-down approach to address the hernia.
The surgeon performs a crossover dissection, staying high in relatively normal tissue, and completes a contralateral dissection before readjusting the ports for a top-down approach.

The surgeon begins a top-down approach by dissecting the tissue on either side of the incisional ventral hernia, ensuring there is no bowel present within the hernia sac before proceeding with further dissection.

The surgeon mobilizes the posterior components, carefully handling the fragile peritoneum along the space of Retzius. Bilateral lower abdominal tars are performed to ensure the peritoneum is securely closed, providing good coverage for the suprapubic defect.

The surgeon anchors the sine core mesh to the pubis using several probing sutures, aiming to maintain mesh integrity and prevent parachuting due to the significant defect and unhealthy tissue in the region.
